content.claim: Special-education teacher (gyógypedagógus) shortages are geographically acute and coincide with historic low in overall public-education teacher numbers: several northeastern districts employ no special-education teachers despite rising SNI-identified children; total public-education/vocational-training teacher count fell ~1,600 from 2022/23 to 2023/24, reaching its lowest level since c. 2000.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: Geographic workforce distribution data cited but not comprehensively mapped
Causal link between teacher shortage and SNI identification rate not quantified
Capacity gap (unable to provide prescribed services) is documented anecdotally but not systematically audited
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
